sections.heading: S
sections.section_value: The patient reports experiencing persistent, sharp chest pain radiating to his left arm, which began approximately 2 hours ago. He states the pain is rated 8/10 and is not relieved by over-the-counter analgesics. He also reports shortness of breath and nausea. The patient denies any recent trauma or history of heart problems.

sections.heading: O
sections.section_value: Blood pressure is 140/90 mmHg. Heart rate is 88 bpm and regular. Oxygen saturation is 98% on room air. Auscultation reveals no murmurs. The patient appears pale and diaphoretic.

sections.heading: A
sections.section_value: The patient's symptoms are concerning for acute myocardial infarction.

sections.heading: P
sections.section_value: Activate EMS and transport to the nearest hospital for immediate evaluation and treatment. Administer oxygen via nasal cannula at 2L/min. Start aspirin 325mg.
